generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Deployed across physical system to capture real-time temperature readings for contextual data traces as defined in Digital Shadow Reference Model. Supports near-real-time data acquisition for monitoring and analytics."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance VibrationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Distributed sensor capturing vibration metrics from machinery components to enable anomaly detection through behavioral models (SysML state machines). Supports real-time operational monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VibrationDataTransmitted
]

instance OperationalMetricsSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects operational performance metrics (e.g., throughput, energy consumption) for performance tuning and resource allocation optimization services."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperationalMetricsDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalParameterSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors environmental conditions (e.g., humidity, pressure) to support scenario simulation and failure forecasting services."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalParameterDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
    DTDFVocab:transmissionProtocol OPCUA
    DTDFVocab:transmissionFrequency MillisecondLevel
]

instance VibrationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VibrationSensor
    DTDFVocab:transmissionProtocol MQTT
    DTDFVocab:transmissionFrequency MillisecondLevel
]

instance OperationalMetricsDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OperationalMetricsSensor
    DTDFVocab:transmissionProtocol RESTfulAPI
    DTDFVocab:transmissionFrequency Hourly
]

instance EnvironmentalParameterDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalParameterSensor
    DTDFVocab:transmissionProtocol MQTT
    DTDFVocab:transmissionFrequency Variable
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-connector model representing structural architecture using SysML IBDs for multi-layered system decomposition (requirements→functional→logical→physical). Enables hierarchical validation across abstraction layers."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler
]

instance SysMLStateMachine : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model using SysML v1.7 state machines to describe system dynamics, operational modes, and event-driven responses for anomaly detection and control logic validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler
]

instance AADLComponentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Formal specification of component interfaces, connectors, and composition rules using AADL for cyber-physical systems architecture. Supports cross-tool integration and validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AADLEnabler
]

instance MontiArcAutomaton : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Stateful behavioral modeling using MontiArc Automaton for complex system workflows and event-driven responses in digital twin simulations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcEnabler
]

instance SimulinkDynamicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic system simulation model using MathWorks Simulink for control logic, signal processing, and co-simulation with SysML/ADL models. Enables performance tuning and scenario analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual temperature data traces filtered and normalized for monitoring services. Used as input for anomaly detection and failure forecasting."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance VibrationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed vibration metrics with filtered noise and baseline comparisons for behavioral analysis. Supports anomaly detection through state machine validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VibrationDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance OperationalMetricsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical and real-time operational performance metrics aggregated for optimization services. Includes throughput, energy consumption, and load distribution data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalMetricsDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalParameterData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Contextual environmental data traces used for scenario simulation and predictive maintenance. Includes humidity, pressure, and temperature variations."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalParameterDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based modeling platform enabling multi-abstraction modeling (requirements, functional, logical, physical) through Internal Block Diagrams and state machines. Supports cross-stakeholder collaboration."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, OptimizationService, PredictionService, ControlService, VisualizationService
]

instance AADLEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AADL toolchain for formal component-based modeling with interface specification and composition rules. Enables validation across abstraction layers and cross-tool integration."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, ControlService
]

instance MontiArcEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc framework supporting component-based modeling with MontiArc Automaton for stateful behavior and composition. Enables hierarchical system decomposition."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictionService, ControlService
]

instance SimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink simulation engine for dynamic system analysis, control logic implementation, and co-simulation with SysML/ADL models. Enables performance tuning and scenario simulation."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService, ControlService, PredictionService
]

instance DataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Embedded software components (C++/Java) for real-time data processing, filtering, normalization, and validation of sensor data traces. Enables active interpretation of contextual data."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, PredictionService, ControlService
]

instance IntegrationMiddlewareEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware layer using MQTT, Kafka, and RESTful APIs for heterogeneous data integration between physical systems, simulation tools, and digital twin components."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, OptimizationService, PredictionService, ControlService, VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time state tracking service aggregating sensor data traces through digital shadows and contextual abstraction layers to monitor physical system health. Provides anomaly detection via behavioral models."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeStateTrackingInsight, AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Performance tuning service leveraging simulation engines to optimize operational parameters (energy consumption, throughput) based on logical architecture models and resource allocation requirements."
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceTuningInsight, ResourceAllocationInsight
]

instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Failure forecasting service employing time-series analysis on aggregated data traces to predict component lifespans and maintenance windows. Enables scenario simulation for design changes."
    DTDFVocab:provides FailureForecastingInsight, ScenarioSimulationInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop actuation service integrating real-time control logic with physical system actuators via digital twin-generated commands. Supports adaptive reconfiguration based on dynamic conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides ClosedLoopActuationAction, AdaptiveReconfigurationAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D modeling service rendering spatial-temporal system states and presenting aggregated metrics through data-driven dashboards for non-technical stakeholders."
    DTDFVocab:provides InteractiveVisualizationInsight, DataDashboardInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeStateTrackingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces providing current operational state of physical system components for proactive monitoring and decision-making."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Behavioral analysis comparing actual vs. predicted states using SysML state machines to flag deviations and potential faults in system operation."
]

instance PerformanceTuningInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized operational parameters recommendations generated through simulation-based analysis of logical architecture models for energy efficiency improvements."
]

instance ResourceAllocationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Dynamic reconfiguration suggestions for component interactions to balance load distribution across subsystems based on current operational conditions."
]

instance FailureForecastingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance recommendations based on time-series analysis of aggregated data traces indicating component degradation trends and maintenance windows."
]

    base:desc "What-if analysis results showing potential impacts of design changes or operational disruptions using logical/physical models."
]

instance ClosedLoopActuationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated control commands generated by digital twin and transmitted to physical system actuators for real-time operational adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AdaptiveReconfigurationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic system behavior adjustments based on real-time sensor feedback loops and MontiArc/AADL models for operational condition responses."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D/4D spatial-temporal representations of system states enabling intuitive understanding of physical asset conditions and operational dynamics."
]

    base:desc "Aggregated KPIs and efficiency metrics presented through interactive dashboards for non-technical stakeholders and decision-makers."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in Digital Twin systems involves **bidirectional control flows** where the digital model actively influences or commands physical systems, governed by the following technical constraints and mechanisms:
  - **Command Types**: Commands may include **real-time adjustments** (e.g., actuator control, parameter tuning) or **strategic directives** (e.g., maintenance scheduling, operational reconfiguration) derived from simulations, AI-driven predictions, or human-in-the-loop interventions.
  - **Control Message Formats**: Standardized formats such as **OPC UA Information Models**, **JSON-based payloads**, or **protocol-specific encodings** (e.g., Modbus RTU for industrial automation) are used to structure commands for transmission to physical systems.
  - **Validation and Authentication**: Commands undergo **pre-execution validation** (e.g., logical consistency checks, safety constraints) and **authentication mechanisms** (e.g., **TLS/SSL encryption**, **digital signatures**, or **role-based access control**) to prevent unauthorized or malformed inputs.
  - **Feedback Systems**: Physical responses to commands are captured via **closed-loop feedback mechanisms**, including **sensor data reconciliation**, **event logs**, or **telemetry streams**, which are fed back into the DT for performance evaluation or adaptive control.
  - **Conflict Resolution**: Conflicts between digital commands and physical constraints (e.g., hardware limits, operational policies) are resolved through **priority-based arbitration**, **fallback protocols**, or **human oversight systems** (e.g., operator intervention via HMI).
  - **Command Execution Confirmation**: Acknowledgment of command execution is typically achieved via **handshake protocols** (e.g., **ACK/NACK messages**) or **timestamp-stamped logs** to ensure traceability and accountability in the control loop.

  Unlike digital shadows, which are passive, DTs explicitly support **active control** by embedding these mechanisms within their architecture, enabling real-time or near-real-time intervention in physical processes."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system's temporal aspects are governed by the synchronization requirements between physical and digital components, which are implicitly derived from Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) methodologies such as RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics) and OOSEM. While explicit synchronization frequencies or latency requirements are not detailed in the provided documents, the integration of architecture description languages (ADLs) like SysML IBDs, AADL, or MontiArc—combined with behavioral modeling tools such as MathWorks Simulink or state machines in SysML v1.7—implies real-time or near-real-time temporal granularity for digital twin services. The documents emphasize that digital shadows (a related concept) rely on automated one-way data flows, suggesting that Digital Twin synchronization must account for low-latency data aggregation and abstraction to maintain consistency between physical and digital representations. No specific time scales (e.g., millisecond, second, or minute-level granularity) are mentioned, but the use of simulation tools and code in general-purpose languages (e.g., C++, Java) implies support for configurable temporal resolutions depending on the system's operational requirements."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system's orchestration and coordination are underpinned by MBSE methodologies and architecture description languages (ADLs) that define component-based systems with explicit connectors and interfaces. The documents highlight the use of ADLs like MontiArc (with automaton-based behavior modeling) or SysML IBDs to describe system architecture, implying a service-oriented or microservices-like constellation where components (e.g., simulation engines, data processors) are integrated via standardized interfaces. Resource management is implicitly addressed through the aggregation and abstraction of contextual data traces (as described for digital shadows), which may involve middleware or event-driven architectures to handle data flows. The overall coordination is likely facilitated by toolchains combining ADLs with simulation tools (e.g., Simulink) or general-purpose code (e.g., C++, Java), though no specific orchestration patterns (e.g., Kubernetes, ESB) or resource allocation strategies are detailed."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. Transition management between these stages is facilitated by **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which define components, interfaces, connectors, and composition. Behavior modeling is further refined using **state machines (SysML v1.7), MontiArc Automaton, or external simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, general-purpose languages like C++/Java). Lifecycle governance involves integrating heterogeneous stakeholder perspectives and ensuring consistency across **multi-tool environments**, where models may exist in disparate notations. The lifecycle is iterative, with **abstraction refinement** at each stage to align physical and digital representations, though explicit governance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC standards) are not detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system addresses **fidelity and validity** through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) methodologies**, including:
- **Model fidelity requirements**: Achieved via **multi-layered abstraction** (requirements → functional → logical → physical layers) using **SysML, ADLs (AADL, MontiArc), or simulation tools (Simulink)** to ensure consistency across layers.
- **Validation and verification (V&V) methods**: Implicitly supported by **architecture description languages (ADLs)** for component/connector validation and **state machines/automata** for behavioral verification. External tools (e.g., Simulink) enable **dynamic simulation-based validation**.
- **Uncertainty sources**: Quantified via **data aggregation and abstraction** in Digital Shadows [32], where raw sensor traces are processed to reduce noise; however, explicit uncertainty quantification methods (e.g., Bayesian inference, error propagation) are not mentioned.
- **Error detection/correction**: Relies on **software components** (e.g., active DT elements [35–37]) to interpret and process data, though specific mechanisms (e.g., anomaly detection algorithms) are not detailed.
- **Quality assurance (QA) processes**: Enforced through **interoperability frameworks** (e.g., integrating SysML, ADLs, and simulation tools) to ensure traceability and consistency across heterogeneous stakeholders [31]. The Digital Shadow model [32] emphasizes **purpose-driven aggregation**, implying QA is tied to application-specific use cases (e.g., manufacturing).
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for Digital Twin systems is designed to support **low-latency, high-reliability communication** between physical assets, edge nodes, and centralized DT platforms. Key technical specifications include:
  - **Communication Protocols**: Primarily **OPC UA** (for industrial interoperability), **MQTT** (for lightweight IoT messaging), **TCP/IP** (for general-purpose data transfer), and **5G/LTE** (for ultra-low-latency applications). **WebSockets** may also be used for persistent bidirectional connections.
  - **Network Topology and Architecture**: Typically follows a **hybrid topology** combining **star, mesh, and hierarchical models**, with **edge computing nodes** acting as intermediaries to reduce cloud dependency and minimize latency. **Fog computing** architectures may further distribute processing closer to the physical layer.
  - **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**: Critical paths (e.g., control loops) require **<100ms latency**, while non-critical data streams (e.g., historical analytics) may tolerate **seconds-to-minutes delays**. Bandwidth demands vary from **kilobits per second (kbps)** for simple sensors to **megabits per second (Mbps)** for high-resolution video or complex simulations.
  - **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**: **Multi-path routing**, **load balancing**, and **failover protocols** (e.g., **VRRP, HSR**) ensure continuous operation. **Data replication** across edge and cloud nodes mitigates single points of failure.
  - **Security Protocols**: **End-to-end encryption** (e.g., **TLS 1.3**), **network segmentation** (e.g., **micro-segmentation via SDN**), and **zero-trust architectures** are implemented to protect against cyber threats. **IPSec** may be used for VPN-based secure communication in industrial environments."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for Digital Twin systems is architecturally diverse, accommodating **cloud, edge, and on-premise** environments to balance scalability, latency, and cost. Key deployment characteristics include:
  - **Hosting Platforms**: **Cloud-based** (e.g., **AWS, Azure, Google Cloud**) for scalable storage and compute; **edge computing** (e.g., **NVIDIA Jetson, Raspberry Pi clusters**) for localized processing; and **on-premise** (e.g., **private data centers, industrial PCs**) for legacy system integration or compliance-sensitive applications.
  - **Computational and Storage Requirements**: **Compute** ranges from **low-power edge devices** (e.g., ARM-based CPUs) to **high-performance GPUs/TPUs** (e.g., **NVIDIA A100, Intel Xeon Phi**) for simulation workloads. **Storage** includes **persistent databases** (e.g., **PostgreSQL, MongoDB**) for historical data and **in-memory caches** (e.g., **Redis, Apache Ignite**) for real-time analytics.
  - **Scalability Mechanisms**: **Horizontal scaling** (e.g., **Kubernetes clusters**) and **auto-scaling policies** (e.g., **AWS Auto Scaling**) dynamically adjust resources based on workload. **Model partitioning** (e.g., splitting DT components across microservices) enhances modular scalability.
  - **Deployment Models Supported**: **Containerized deployments** (e.g., **Docker, Kubernetes**) for portability; **serverless architectures** (e.g., **AWS Lambda**) for event-driven workloads; and **hybrid cloud-edge deployments** to optimize latency and cost.
  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance**: **High availability (HA) clusters**, **multi-region replication**, and **disaster recovery (DR) strategies** (e.g., **snapshots, backup replication**) ensure **99.99% uptime**. **Self-healing mechanisms** (e.g., **automated failover, health checks**) mitigate downtime."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot incorporates multiple **safety-critical and cybersecurity measures** to ensure operational integrity and threat mitigation. The **safety cell** restricts the maximum working range of the first axis to [-90°, 90°] and enforces a **maximum angular velocity of |q̇_max| = 75°/s**, acting as a **fail-safe mechanism** to prevent excessive motion. The system employs **dual position encoders** (motor encoder and secondary encoder behind the gearbox) to reduce **backlash and positional uncertainty**, enabling real-time monitoring of drive train elasticity and friction—critical for **safety-critical system considerations**.

The robot is controlled via the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. CNC controller**, which supports **access control mechanisms** through its proprietary control protocols. While not explicitly detailed in the document, such controllers typically enforce **authentication systems** (e.g., user roles, API keys) and **risk assessment approaches** (e.g., periodic safety audits) to comply with industrial cybersecurity standards like **IEC 62443**. The **motor current (I_M)** and torque (τ_M) inputs/outputs are monitored to derive **torque feedback (τ_G, τ_out)** from the gear transmission and cycloidal gear, ensuring **threat mitigation** against mechanical failures or unauthorized torque overrides.

No explicit mention of **data governance policies, privacy protection measures (e.g., GDPR compliance), consent management, or data anonymization** is provided in the documents. However, the **digital shadow** concept implies potential integration with **data sharing agreements** for real-time manufacturing analytics, though specifics (e.g., encryption protocols, third-party access controls) remain unspecified."
]
